title: XPath Injection
principle: Don't build an XPath expression by concatenating user input. Use a precompiled expression with bound variables (a variable resolver), so the input is a value the evaluator substitutes, never part of the query syntax.

snippets.note: Bind $name through the resolver; the value can't alter the expression. Never string-concatenate into the XPath.
lxml substitutes named variables as values, not syntax. Do the same with ElementTree by filtering results in Python rather than embedding input in the path.
Provide the value through an XsltContext/variable rather than interpolating. For simple cases, validate the input against a strict allowlist first.
